value: 일본 국외 거주 외국인 여권 소지자. 'Temporary Visitor' 체류자격으로 입국한 자만 구매 가능. 일본 국적자 또는 일본 거주 외국인은 구매 불가.

value.비고: 2023년 10월 인상 후 유지. 2025-2026 추가 인상 없음.

value: 한국 국적자는 90일 이내 관광 목적 무비자 입국 가능. 여권 잔여 유효기간 제한 없음 (체류 기간 이상 권장). Visit Japan Web 사전 등록 권장.
Suica/PASMO 등 IC카드로 전철, 버스, 편의점 결제 가능. 물리 카드 재고 부족은 2025.3월 공식 해소. Welcome Suica(관광객용, 28일 유효) 주요 역에서 판매 재개. 모바일 Suica(Apple/Google) 및 Welcome Suica Mobile(iPhone, 180일 유효) 이용 가능.
지정석(Reserved)과 자유석(Non-reserved) 구분. JR Pass 소지 시 지정석 무료 예약 가능 (Nozomi/Mizuho 제외). 자유석은 선착순 탑승.
면세 범위: 주류 3병(760ml 이하), 담배 200개비(거주자)/400개비(비거주자), 향수 2oz, 기타 품목 20만엔 이하. 초과 시 신고 및 관세 부과.

value.비고: 구급차 무료, 치료비는 별도. 119/110은 일본어 우선이나 통역 연결 가능.

value: Visit Japan Web은 웹 기반 서비스(앱 불필요). 여권 정보, 입국심사(하선카드), 세관신고, 면세쇼핑 3개 항목 사전 등록. 등록 후 QR코드 발급 → 공항 도착 시 제시. 2026년 현재 필수는 아니나 강력 권장 (심사 시간 대폭 단축). 스크린샷 불가, 라이브 접속 필요.

신제도_2026_11월.방식: 환급형 (구매 시 세금 포함 → 출국 시 환급)

value: As of 2025, Japan grants visa-free entry to citizens of 74 countries and regions for short-term tourism.
Travelers may import items duty-free if the overseas market value is under 10,000 yen and there is no duty-free allowance for articles valued over 200,000 yen each or each set.
Visitors defined as non-residents will be eligible to use Japan’s new tax refund system starting November 1, 2026.
Travelers must confirm whether they need a visa to enter Japan before registering on Visit Japan Web; if they have a visa, they can copy details from their visa application during registration.
Japan will introduce a policy requiring all incoming tourists to have private medical insurance starting in 2025 to cover potentially high medical costs during emergencies.
A filling set meal in large business districts or busy railway stations in Japan can cost around 1000 yen, and lunch boxes can be around 500 yen
Local residents pay 1,000 yen ($7.01), visitors from outside the city pay 2,500 yen ($17.52)
Train ride costs 420 yen, access to main temple is 200 yen
Admission fee is 620 yen
Admission and 24 Hour Subway Ticket costs $44
Immersive Art Experience Ticket costs $27
In 2018, cash payments made up 82% of all payments in Japan
The average price for a private room in a hostel or guesthouse in Japan is $32 per night
Single-entry visa fee is $20.00 (general), $6.00 for Indian passport holders
Visit Japan Web registration is completely free of charge
Using an ambulance in Japan is free of charge, even for international visitors.
Items not listed can be duty-free if their total value is under 200,000 yen; items exceeding 200,000 yen are subject to a 15% duty plus a 10% consumption tax
Purchases of 1,000,000 JPY (tax-exclusive) or more require detailed product registration for tax-free handling and customs verification
From July 2026, Japan will raise its international departure tax from JPY 1,000 to JPY 3,000 per person for travellers leaving the country by air or sea.

As of 2026, many stations are reducing staffed ticket counters, making IC cards like Suica useful to save travelers time. Suica can be used either as a physical IC card or via Mobile Suica on smartphones. Suica purchased in Tokyo can be used nationwide due to Japan’s IC card interoperability system, including subways in Osaka and buses in Fukuoka. Standard physical Suica cards (not Welcome Suica) can be refunded before leaving Japan at JR East ticket offices called Midori no Madoguchi.

The Japan Rail Pass is valid for all railways of the JR Group, including Shinkansen super express trains except 'NOZOMI' and 'MIZUHO', limited express trains, express trains, and rapid or local trains.

To use a Japan SIM card, you must understand your visa status as it affects eligibility and required documentation; prepare your residence card with your Japanese address; setting up a Japanese bank account facilitates payments especially for postpaid contracts; prioritize providers with strong English support; and complete the contract and device setup as guided.

Sparks eSIM provides unlimited data and seamless access to both international and local communication platforms, with partnerships with leading Japanese carriers ensuring priority access to high-speed networks and seamless network transitions during high-speed rail travel.
Many hotels in Japan, especially ryokans, require guests to remove their shoes at the entrance or in their rooms.
Ryokans are traditional Japanese inns offering an immersive cultural experience, often featuring tatami mats, sliding doors, yukata robes, and kaiseki multi-course dinners.
Shibuya, Tokyo

Using Sparks eSIM with unlimited data ensures seamless access to both international and local communication platforms, with priority access to high-speed networks and seamless network transitions during high-speed rail travel.
Prepaid SIM cards can be bought at some convenience stores and electronics shops in Japan for travelers who didn't purchase a SIM card at the airport.
Wi-FiRental.com offers pocket Wi-Fi rental plans in Japan with K4/Wimax/SoftBank plans available, priced at ¥6,600/month with a discount code 'cheapo' for free ¥1,100 at checkout.
Typical dinner time in Japan generally falls between 6:00 p.m. and 8:00 p.m., though this can vary depending on individual household routines and work or school schedules.
Kinmata, Kyoto, Japan
Kaiseki is Japan’s most refined dining, almost synonymous with Western haute cuisine, and requires the highest level of etiquette and respect for the food.
In Japan, it is generally a good idea to make a reservation in advance for popular restaurants, unless the restaurant does not accept bookings.
Most IC cards require a security deposit of 500 yen when purchasing the card.
The 10 most popular IC cards, including Suica, Pasmo, and Icoca, are compatible with each other and can be used on almost all train, subway, and bus lines inside Japanese cities.

When selecting a payment service in Japan, it is fundamental to check whether it accepts major credit card brands such as Visa and Mastercard, as well as payment options widely used in South Korea and China, the major home countries for travelers to Japan.
Japan uses many international payment methods such as Apple Pay and Google Pay, and domestic payment methods like PayPay are popular for QR code payments.
The Tokyo to Kyoto Shinkansen can be covered in just over two hours.
A taxi ride from Haneda Airport to Tokyo takes around 30-40 minutes
The Japan Rail Pass is highly recommended as a travel companion for riding Shinkansen bullet trains and other rail services, offering access to Green Car reservations and specific seat selections.
The subway system in Tokyo stops running at midnight and resumes at 5 AM
Most retail shops and department stores open around 10:00 AM and close around 8:00 PM
